title: Hymenostephium websteri
intro: Hymenostephium websteri, a plant species native to Mexico, is a type of shrub in the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Hymenostephium websteri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Hymenostephium websteri is classified within the family Compositae. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and its relationships to other organisms. Within the family Compositae, Hymenostephium websteri is placed in the subfamily Asteroideae. The subfamily Asteroideae is further divided into several tribes, and Hymenostephium websteri is a member of the tribe Vernonieae. This classification helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
